title: iPhone 17e vs Galaxy S25 FE vs Pixel 10a: Specs, features, India pricing and more compared
summary: Apple launched the iPhone 17e with an A19 processor, priced under ₹70,000. Competing with Pixel 10a and Galaxy S25 FE, it features a 6.1-inch display, IP68 rating, and a single 48MP camera. The Pixel 10a offers better battery and price, while the S25 FE has a more versatile camera setup.

title: Apple Launch event 2026: iPhone 17e price starts at ₹64,990, upgrades base storage to 256 GB for budget buyers
summary: &nbsp;The device features the A19 chip and MagSafe support. Pre-orders begin Wednesday, with sales starting March 11, as Apple aims to attract new users amid rising memory chip prices.

title: Is Claude down? Hundreds of users report issues in Delhi, Mumbai, Ahmedabad; Anthropic reacts
summary: Claude, Anthropic's AI chatbot, experienced service disruptions on Monday, affecting cities like Delhi and Mumbai. Downdetector reported issues related to Claude.ai and login/logout paths, while Anthropic confirmed that the Claude API is functioning correctly.

title: Stop buying gaming laptops for trading: Your trading laptop must have these specs to handle trading software
summary: Professional trading requires single-core CPU speed and Thunderbolt 4, not gaming GPUs. This guide reveals the specs that eliminate chart lag and slippage, ensuring your hardware matches the market speed.

title: Apple Launch Event 2026 LIVE Updates: iPhone 17e unveiled at $599, pre-orders open March 4
summary: Apple Launch LIVE Updates: Apple's big week of launches is all set to kick off from today. The company could unveil new MacBooks, iPads and the iPhone 17e this week.

title: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion full specifications revealed ahead of launch: here's what to expect
summary: Motorola has unveiled the Edge 70 Fusion at MWC in Barcelona. The devices features a 6.8-inch AMOLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, 7,000mAh battery, and dual rear cameras.

title: AI+ Pulse 2 with 120Hz display and 6,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at ₹5,999
summary: AI+ has unveiled the Pulse 2 smartphone featuring an octa-core Unisoc T7250 processor, up to 6GB RAM, and a 6,000mAh battery. It runs on NxtQuantum OS based on Android 16 and will be available starting March 11 for ₹5,999.

title: March smartphone launches: Xiaomi 17 series, Nothing Phone 4a, iPhone 17e and more
summary: February saw numerous smartphone launches, including Samsung's S26 lineup and iQOO 15R. March promises even more launches with the iPhone 17e, Xiaomi 17 series, Nothing Phone 4a series, and more.&nbsp;

title: Amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend, Anthropic launches feature to help users switch to Claude
summary: Anthropic has launched a new import memory feature for Claude, which allows paid users to bring memories from other AI chatbots. The feature comes at a time when Anthropic has seen strong public sentiment in its favour following the cancellation of its deal with the US government.

title: Claude down: Second outage in 24 hours, affects users across regions
summary: Anthropic’s Claude experienced its second outage in less than 24 hours. Over 4,000 US users and around 300 in India reported problems, mostly with Claude chat. The app and website were also affected. The company is investigating the issue.

title: Macquarie Asset Management, Gabia to invest $410 million in South Korean data centre platform
summary: As per the agreement signed on February 25, ​Macquarie Group and Gabia are set to ⁠invest over ‌the next ​four ​to six years. The platform is targeting more than 100 megawatts ​of data centre capacity across key ​metropolitan and regional locations in South Korea.

title: OpenAI amending deal with Pentagon to ensure surveillance safeguards: CEO Sam Altman
summary: Sam Altman said OpenAI is working with the United States Department of Defense to clarify their agreement. The deal states that services will not be used by intelligence agencies such as the National Security Agency without a contract change. OpenAI recently secured a classified network deal.

title: Pronto raises $25 million from Epiq Capital, existing backers
summary: Pronto has raised $25 million in a round led by Epiq Capital, with participation from existing investors Glade Brook Capital, General Catalyst, and Bain Capital Ventures, Anjali Sardana, the company’s founder and CEO, told ET. With this, Pronto has a post-money valuation of $100 million.
